authoritative_sources.title: FSMA Final Rule on Requirements for Additional Traceability Records for Certain Foods
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's traceability final rule is the authoritative U.S. public baseline for critical tracking events, traceability plans, lot codes, and key data elements when food CPG launch work depends on batch and lot evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: launch intake, lot assignment, transformation, shipping, and receiving evidence review
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains scenarios involving lot mismatch, launch holds, and low-confidence handling when traceability records or compliance timing are unclear.

authoritative_sources.title: Traceability Lot Code
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's traceability lot code guidance is the authoritative public operating reference for how a traceability lot stays linked to other key data elements through execution and distribution events.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: lot-level launch execution, record linkage, and closure evidence
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Used to define how completion records and blocked-launch summaries must reference lot-linked evidence rather than generic SKU-level statements.

authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 117 - Current Good Manufacturing Practice, Hazard Analysis, and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 21 CFR Part 117 is the primary public regulatory source for food manufacturing, packing, holding, records, corrective actions, and supply-chain program requirements that bound launch execution behavior.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: manufacturing execution, records review, hold posture, corrective action, and supply-chain verification
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains lifecycle progression and exception handling so the lane does not treat incomplete manufacturing, holding, or corrective-action evidence as sufficient for completion.

authoritative_sources.title: FSMA Final Rule for Preventive Controls for Human Food
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's Preventive Controls for Human Food summary is an authoritative operating reference for supply-chain program, verification, documentation, and preventive-controls workflow expectations under FSMA.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: launch readiness, supplier verification dependency review, and documented preventive-controls checks
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Used to distinguish what launch execution can summarize from what formal preventive-controls or quality owners must approve.

authoritative_sources.title: FSMA Final Rule on Foreign Supplier Verification Programs (FSVP) for Importers of Food for Humans and Animals
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's FSVP rule is the authoritative public reference for risk-based supplier verification activities when imported materials or components affect launch readiness and supplier-boundary decisions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: supplier status review and imported-material escalation
used_for: escalation when supplier approval is missing
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Used in supplier substitution and missing-approval scenarios so the lane routes supplier qualification and verification to the correct specialist owners.

authoritative_sources.title: Initiation of Voluntary Recalls Under 21 CFR Part 7, Subpart C
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA recall guidance is an authoritative operating reference for stop-ship, market withdrawal, and escalation boundaries when launch inventory or shipped product enters recall handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: hold, release-stop, and downstream escalation review
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains hold and release scenarios so launch execution stops and escalates rather than improvising recall or release authority.

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 Global Traceability Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 is the recognized standards body for cross-enterprise identifiers and traceability exchange used widely across CPG manufacturing, warehousing, retail, and supplier ecosystems.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: cross-system lot, shipment, and chain-of-custody traceability review
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Used to define what evidence a blocked-launch summary or completion record must preserve across ERP, MES, WMS, and external partner systems.

authoritative_sources.title: EPCIS & CBV
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 EPCIS and CBV are authoritative public standards for event capture and visibility data, including what, when, where, why, and how details across supply-chain movement and status changes.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: inventory movement, launch event capture, and chain-of-custody evidence handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Used to frame traceability-conflict and completion-evidence scenarios around event records instead of generic narrative summaries.

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 GDSN standards
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 GDSN is the authoritative public framework for synchronised product and packaging master data used by brand owners, suppliers, and retailers for launch-ready product setup.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: item setup, pack-data alignment, and trading-partner readiness
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Used for launch adaptation scenarios where product setup, pallet labels, or retailer routing rules depend on synchronized item data and explicit tenant overlays.

workflow_stages.name: launch intake and readiness framing
workflow_stages.description: Review the accepted launch order, production order, product scope, line assignment, release authority, and minimum prerequisite records before execution begins.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: execution-state progression and dependency validation
workflow_stages.description: Progress launch work through staged execution only when production, inventory, lot, supplier, and quality dependencies are documented in authoritative systems of record.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: exception, rework, and blocked-state handling
workflow_stages.description: Handle line stops, quality exceptions, record conflicts, and material shortages through explicit blocked or rework states rather than silent progression.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: completion evidence and downstream release dependency review
workflow_stages.description: Prepare completion or fulfillment records only when closeout evidence, lot linkage, inventory state, and release dependencies are documented and do not require external authority decisions.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: tenant-specific launch adaptation and routing
workflow_stages.description: Apply plant-, co-manufacturer-, and retailer-specific overlays while preserving the public baseline and escalating whenever tenant rules or public sources materially conflict.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

artifacts: launch orders, production orders, startup checklists, and line clearance records
batch and lot genealogy, traceability lot codes, inventory reservations, and warehouse pick records
quality holds, deviations, nonconformance reports, CAPA references, and release records
supplier records, approved-supplier status, substitution requests, and inbound inspection records
product and packaging master data, pallet labeling instructions, and retailer routing requirements
completion checks, shipment readiness records, and blocked-launch or rework summaries
systems_of_record: traceability repositories or EPCIS-capable event stores
product master-data systems and retailer routing repositories

allowed: classify launch work as in-scope, blocked, rework, complete, or out-of-scope based on documented execution state and evidence
summarize authoritative production, lot, inventory, supplier, and quality records into execution status updates and completion or exception packages
identify missing prerequisites, conflicting records, and next owners without overstating approval authority
escalate: final quality release, hold disposition, deviation closure, or recall action
supplier approval, alternate-source approval, or formal substitution decision
engineering, formulation, packaging-spec, or regulated-labeling approval
legal or policy interpretation when public and tenant rules materially conflict
refuse: mark work complete without completion evidence tied to line, lot, and release state
bypass lot traceability, release, or quality controls to protect schedule
approve supplier substitution or engineering change without documented authority

domain_failure_modes: launch work progresses using unreconciled batch, lot, or inventory records across ERP, MES, and WMS
completion or shipment is treated as valid while the affected lot remains on quality hold or unresolved deviation status
supplier shortage or substitution pressure is treated as an execution-only problem instead of a supplier-quality or change-control decision
blocked or rework states are skipped to satisfy launch timing, creating unsafe release or audit gaps
tenant-specific routing, pack-data, or release rules are guessed without authoritative tenant retrieval
the lane drifts into engineering, supplier approval, or regulatory signoff authority

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Supplier or substitution decision outside lane authority
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: FDA food traceability compliance date messaging
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: FDA public materials reviewed on April 9, 2026 still include a January 20, 2026 compliance date, while a later FDA 2025 proposed extension would move the date to July 20, 2028 if finalized.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Treat this as a nonblocking but material timing ambiguity: runtime must require tenant regulatory confirmation when the compliance date affects launch workflow or evidence obligations rather than assuming one date.

unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Cosmetics, household chemical, and other non-food CPG launches may rely on different product-safety and release regimes than FDA food rules.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant-specific product-family overlays and regulated-lane routing for non-food lines while preserving GS1 traceability and execution-state discipline as the shared baseline.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Facility-specific supplier approval and co-manufacturer routing
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Supplier qualification authority and co-manufacturing ownership often differ by tenant contract, facility model, and imported-material exposure.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant retrieval of supplier-approval matrices, co-manufacturer SOPs, and facility authority maps before high-confidence supplier or release routing.

notes: Authoritative launch-execution records are tenant-designated ERP, MES, WMS, QMS, supplier, and master-data records constrained by FDA and GS1 public baselines where applicable.
The lane stops before final quality release, supplier approval, engineering signoff, and formal regulatory interpretation even when launch timing pressure is high.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated packaging because each open ambiguity is explicitly routed to tenant retrieval or human resolution instead of silent synthesis.
